user.title: Add Airport
user.description: Select the continent from which you want to add an airport.

country.title: Select Country
country.description: Select the country from which you want to add an airport.

airport.title: Select Airport
airport.description: Select the airport you want to monitor.

related_objects.title: Related Objects
related_objects.description:
A related Flight Information Region (FIR) was detected for the selected airport. SIGMET weather warnings are issued per FIR, not per airport.

Detected: **{fir_label}**

data_description.add_fir: Creates a separate device for the FIR. If you add more airports from the same FIR later, the existing device will be reused.

error.airport_exists: This airport has already been added.

abort.already_configured: This entry has already been configured.

init.title: Airport Settings
init.description: Select any additional entities you would like to enable, and adjust how often data is fetched.

data_description.enabled_entities: The primary METAR sensor (flight category, raw report) is always created. Enable additional sensors for individual weather parameters, or the TAF forecast sensor.
data_description.metar_interval: How often to fetch current weather conditions (METAR). Routine METAR reports are issued every 30 minutes; 10 minutes is a good default to catch rapid SPECI updates.
data_description.taf_interval: How often to fetch the forecast (TAF). TAF reports are issued every 6 hours; 30 minutes is more than sufficient.
data_description.add_fir: Creates a FIR device for **{fir_label}**. SIGMET alerts will appear on this device. If you add more airports from the same FIR later, this device will be reused.

fir_init.title: FIR Settings
fir_init.description: Select any additional SIGMET entities you would like to enable, and adjust how often SIGMET data is fetched for **{fir_icao}**.

data_description.enabled_entities: The primary SIGMET sensor (active/inactive, full advisory details) is always created. Enable additional sensors for the active SIGMET count, hazards, and latest valid-until time.
data_description.sigmet_interval: How often to fetch SIGMET data. SIGMETs are issued as needed rather than on a fixed schedule; 15 minutes is a good default to catch new or amended advisories promptly.

stale_metar.title: METAR data for {airport} hasn't updated in a while
stale_metar.description:
Aviation Weather hasn't been able to fetch a current METAR report for **{airport}** for several hours. This usually means a connectivity problem between Home Assistant and aviationweather.gov, or that the airport has stopped publishing METAR reports.

Check **Settings → System → Logs** (filtered to "aviation_weather") for details. This issue clears automatically once data starts flowing again.

stale_sigmet.title: SIGMET data for {fir} hasn't updated in a while
stale_sigmet.description:
Aviation Weather hasn't been able to fetch SIGMET data for **{fir}** for several hours — this is separate from simply having no active SIGMET, which is normal. This usually means a connectivity problem between Home Assistant and aviationweather.gov.

Check **Settings → System → Logs** (filtered to "aviation_weather") for details. This issue clears automatically once data starts flowing again.
